concepts.lesson: Uniform motion is a type of motion where an object moves with constant speed and direction. This means that the object covers equal distances in equal intervals of time. For example, if a car travels 100 km in 2 hours, it is moving at a uniform speed.
key_points: Object covers equal distances in equal intervals of time
Example: Car traveling 100 km in 2 hours

concepts.lesson: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity. It can be positive, negative, or zero. When an object accelerates, its velocity changes in magnitude or direction. For example, when a car accelerates from rest to 60 km/h, it has undergone acceleration.
key_points: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity
Velocity can change in magnitude or direction
Example: Car accelerating from rest to 60 km/h

concepts.lesson: Displacement is the shortest distance between the initial and final positions of an object. It can be negative, positive, or zero depending on the direction of motion. For example, if a person walks 5 meters east and then 3 meters west, their displacement is 2 meters east.
key_points: Displacement is the shortest distance between initial and final positions
Can be negative, positive, or zero depending on direction of motion
Example: Person walking 5 meters east and then 3 meters west
